frameworks.name: Total Addressable Market Analysis
frameworks.description: Framework for sizing the market opportunity
steps: 1. Define the market - what problem is being solved?
2. Calculate top-down TAM - total potential customers x average revenue per customer
3. Validate with bottom-up - build from current customers and expand
4. Identify SAM (Serviceable) - realistic portion they can address
5. Project SOM (Obtainable) - what they can realistically capture
red_flags: TAM calculated on overly broad market definitions
No clear path from current position to TAM capture

frameworks.name: PEG Ratio Framework
frameworks.description: Peter Lynch's approach to valuing growth stocks
frameworks.formula: PEG = P/E Ratio / Earnings Growth Rate
caveats: Works best for consistent growers (15-25% range)
Less useful for hyper-growth or declining companies
Should be combined with quality assessment
sources: Peter Lynch's One Up on Wall Street

frameworks.name: Ten-Bagger Identification
frameworks.description: Lynch's criteria for stocks that could return 10x
characteristics: Small to mid-cap with room to grow (market cap < $10B ideal)

frameworks.name: Network Effects Assessment
frameworks.description: Evaluate the strength and type of network effects
types: Direct network effects: More users = more value per user (social networks)
Indirect network effects: More of one type attracts another (platforms)
Data network effects: More usage = better product (AI, recommendations)
Marketplace network effects: Buyers attract sellers and vice versa

frameworks.name: Revenue Quality Assessment
frameworks.description: Evaluate the sustainability and predictability of revenue growth

hierarchy.highest_quality: Recurring subscription revenue with high retention (>90% NRR)
hierarchy.high_quality: Consumption-based revenue with predictable usage patterns
hierarchy.medium_quality: Repeat transactional revenue from loyal customers
hierarchy.lower_quality: One-time project revenue or lumpy deals

metrics: Cohort analysis - do older customers spend more?
